code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test22-APR-2022Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low CO₂, high chloride, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance. These findings suggest impaired renal function, possible tubular dysfunction, and metabolic derangement. Low HbA1c argues against chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation to assess for acute or chronic kidney disease and further investigate the etiology of renal and metabolic abnormalities.

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
